title: France Grants ITER Vacuum Vessel Key Regulatory Exemption from Pressure Rules
detail: On April 14, 2026, France's nuclear safety authority (ASNR — Autorité de sûreté nucléaire et de radioprotection) approved exempting ITER's vacuum vessel from French and European pressure equipment regulations. The ruling recognizes that electromagnetic forces — not coolant pressure — determine the structural design requirements of a tokamak vacuum vessel, making conventional pressure equipment standards inapplicable. The vessel will remain subject to nuclear safety oversight as a Protection Important Component. ITER Director-General Pietro Barabaschi stated the decision would 'contribute to the consolidation of appropriate technical standards for tokamak magnetic confinement fusion devices,' facilitating project progress while maintaining robust safety requirements. The ruling represents a significant step toward developing fusion-specific regulatory frameworks — distinct from standards derived from fission reactor practice — and sets a precedent for future fusion projects globally.

media.caption: ITER vacuum vessel at Cadarache — French regulator grants pressure equipment exemption

title: UK Unveils National Fusion Energy Strategy with £1.3 Billion Commitment
detail: On April 14, 2026, the UK government unveiled its National Fusion Energy Strategy, backed by £1.3 billion in government investment, at Fusion Fest 2026 in London. The strategy charts a path from laboratory to commercial grid power centered on the STEP (Spherical Tokamak for Energy Production) programme at West Burton A, Nottinghamshire, targeting operations in 2040 and approximately 100 MWe net output. Key partnerships announced include: the ILIOS Consortium as STEP Construction Partner (£200 million contract); Tokamak Energy for high-temperature superconducting magnet technology; and Dassault Systèmes for digital systems management. The strategy is part of the broader £2.5 billion UK fusion investment committed for 2025–2030. UK Energy Security Secretary stated the strategy leverages over 70 years of UK fusion research expertise and positions Britain as a global leader in the emerging commercial fusion market. The funding also covers UKAEA operations, the Fusion Futures skills pipeline, and JET decommissioning research at Culham.

media.caption: UK National Fusion Energy Strategy launched at Fusion Fest 2026
